title: Task Compiler LLM Handoff Contract
description: Defines how an LLM (Claude/Codex) produces a structured handoff from free-form issue text into validated task JSON. Captures extraction metadata, field-level confidence, validation gate results, and the exit signal that lets the orchestrator hand off to deterministic compilation. Aligned with docs/ai-native/llm-assisted-task-compiler.md.

sourceType.description: What the LLM was given as input. issue-body = free-form markdown from GitHub. structured-json = CONTROL APPENDIX or pre-structured JSON. hybrid = both.

sourceIssue.description: GitHub issue number that was processed.

sourceChecksum.description: SHA-256 prefix (first 12 chars) of the raw input text. Used for idempotency — re-extraction on the same input should produce the same checksum.

promptHandoff.description: LLM-generated concise summary of what to build and why.

knowledgeRefs.description: File paths or URLs the LLM identified as relevant context.

attentionFocus.description: Key constraints or priorities extracted from the issue.

knownBlindspots.description: Patterns or mistakes the LLM identified to avoid.

complexityAssessment.description: LLM's complexity assessment of the task.

extractionResult.description: Fields extracted by the LLM from the issue input. Required fields mirror the task-v2 structural requirements.

fieldConfidence.description: Confidence level for each extracted field. deterministic = copied verbatim from structured input. high = clearly stated in issue. medium = inferred from context. low = guessed or defaulted.

passed.description: True when all required fields pass validation. False when any field fails.

message.description: Human-readable explanation for fail or warn status.

humanReviewRequired.description: True when any field has low confidence or a validation gate failed. The orchestrator must route to human review before task execution.

validationGate.description: Deterministic validation gate applied after LLM extraction. Mirrors the compiler's specificity checks.

exitSignal.description: Controls the orchestrator's next action. ready = handoff is valid, proceed to task-v2 compilation. needs-human-review = confidence or validation issues require human approval. fallback-to-deterministic = LLM extraction failed; use structured input only. blocked = hard validation failure, cannot proceed.

fallbackPayload.description: When exitSignal is fallback-to-deterministic, contains the partial structural fields the LLM managed to extract before failure. The deterministic compiler uses these as overrides for the default pass-through path.

extractedFields.description: List of field names the LLM successfully extracted before fallback.

failureReason.description: Why the LLM extraction fell back to deterministic.

dryRun.description: When true, the handoff is for review only — no task JSON is compiled or dispatched. All write-capable automation defaults to dry-run.

capturedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the handoff was produced.

llmModel.description: Model identifier that performed the extraction (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6, codex-mini). For audit trail only.

extractionDurationMs.description: Wall-clock milliseconds the LLM extraction took. Used for telemetry and cost estimation.
